Video.summary: How well do you know your neighbors, your friends or even your child's teacher? An estimated four to eight million people in North America are swinging in the suburbs " mostly middle-class folks from all walks of life. Dr. Phil takes you inside the secret world of swingers, a lifestyle where couples swap partners.
Video.title: Secrets in the Suburbs

Video.summary: t’s been almost four months since 5-year-old Summer Wells vanished from her home in rural Tennessee on June 15, 2021. Her parents, Don and Candus, say they believe their daughter was abducted. But, the couple say online bullies have accused them of having something to do with Summer’s disappearance. Don and Candus agreed to sit down with body language experts Scott Rouse and Greg Hartley, interrogators who have worked with the FBI, law enforcement and the military and have been referred to as a human lie detector, in hopes of clearing their name. Find out what the men think and if Dr. Phil agrees with their findings.
Video.title: Summer Wells Vanishes (Part 1)

Video.summary: Who will Brad choose: his wife or girlfriend?
Video.title: Torn Between 2 Lovers Part 1

Video.summary: The family of Shanda Sharer who was murdered confronts one of the convicted killers.
Video.title: In Cold Blood Pt. 2

Video.summary: From marital spats to parenting disagreements, learn the biggest mistakes you're making.
Video.title: Top 5 Mistakes You’re Making

Video.summary: Do you have a nosy loved one in your life snooping through your things, questioning your every move and driving you crazy? Dr. Phil has advice.
Video.title: Mind Your Business

Video.summary: If you think pretty people are immune to heartache, think again. The headlines are filled with stories of striking women who were devastated by infidelity, despite their outward beauty. Dr. Phil sits down with Joey, a husband and father, who says he can’t pass up a pretty face. His wife, Shannon, says he’s slept with close to 100 women since their wedding day. The personal trainer justifies his actions by saying Shannon is overweight and not hot enough for him. Will he get a wake-up call about his cheating ways? Or will his wife take him back yet again? See the shocking text messages to Joey’s mistress, and hear Dr. Phil’s strong message for the hapless husband when a disturbing secret is revealed. Can Dr. Phil encourage Joey to choose another path and look beyond the physical?
Video.title: Beauty Isn’t Enough

Video.summary: Shara claims that her newlywed husband, Luke, is a “mean, insecure know-it-all” who won’t let her own a cell phone, hold a job or leave the house alone -- and also dictates how she should dress and wear her makeup. Luke denies all of Shara’s allegations and claims that she is “no angel” and has a vicious temper. Can this marriage be saved?
Video.title: Trapped By My Controlling Husband

Video.summary: Jaime says she divorced her allegedly abusive husband, Jay, after two years of marriage but says he refuses to let her go and lures her back with promises to change. Jay says Jaime pushes his buttons until he blows up. Can this couple save their relationship for the sake of their child, or are they better off apart?
Video.title: Should They Redo Their I Dos?

Image.alt: Should They Redo Their I Dos?
Should They Redo Their I Dos?
Should They Redo Their I Dos?
Should They Redo Their I Dos?
Should They Redo Their I Dos?
Should They Redo Their I Dos?
Should They Redo Their I Dos?

Video.summary: For many girls, a fairytale wedding day is a sweet fantasy -- but getting the proposal can be a real nightmare! Amber and Rich are struggling through commitment issues. They live, work and have children together, and say they're happy but can't seem to take the next step. Rich sold Amber on his plan to marry her after five years, so why hasn't he proposed after nearly seven? Does he have valid reasons for resisting marriage? After Dr. Phil challenges him, will Rich decide to walk down the aisle ... on Dr. Phil's stage? Next, Jill has taken the leap and been divorced seven times. Why does she pick the wrong men, and what can she do to make her next union last? Then, Candy describes her first marriage as lasting 18 miserable years, and she regrets how she poisoned her daughters against men during that time. What will it take to change her daughter, who calls herself the Runaway Bride? Plus, a don't-miss moment happens in Dr. Phil's audience!
Video.title: How to Get to I Do

Image.alt: How to Get to I Do
How to Get to I Do
How to Get to I Do
How to Get to I Do
How to Get to I Do
How to Get to I Do
How to Get to I Do

Video.summary: Living with an alcoholic parent is one of the most difficult and heart-wrenching experiences a child can go through. Heather, 28, and Alexandra, 21, say their mother, Joey, is a pathetic drunk, and if she doesn't get clean once and for all, they will walk away from her forever.
Video.title: Sober Up Or Else!

Video.summary: Every day, many teen girls fear for their lives because of obsessive, controlling and abusive boyfriends, and it’s another reason why Dr. Phil is so passionate about his campaign to End the Silence on Domestic Violence. Seventeen-year-old Samantha sent an urgent plea for help to Dr. Phil. She says her ex-boyfriend, Aaron, called her nonstop for weeks and sent her threatening text messages after she broke up with him. After Samantha changed her number, he began calling her mother repeatedly. Samantha says he showed up unannounced at her house and pounded on the door for 45 minutes while she cowered in a locked closet. She says she’s afraid Aaron could even kill her. Dr. Phil sits down with the two teens to help Samantha get her message to Aaron loud and clear. And, Tiffany, 20, has a cautionary tale of survival every teen needs to hear. It could save her life!
Video.title: Teens Obsessed With Love

Video.summary: A mother describes her daughter's self-destructive behaviors.
Video.title: 16 and on the Path of Destruction

Image.alt: 16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction
16 and on the Path of Destruction

Video.summary: Sixty-four-year-old Josie says she met the love of her life, Victor, on Facebook. Even though he’s Nigerian, 30 years younger, and she’s never physically met him, she insists it’s true love. She’s purchased a ticket to Nigeria and hopes to marry Victor, but her older sister, Carmen, her best friend, Angela, and her 19-year-old son, Anthony, say Josie is acting like a teenage fool. They believe Victor is conning Josie into marrying him for a green card. Dr. Phil took cameras to Nigeria to find Victor to see if he’s telling Josie the truth. Josie’s family says they want Dr. Phil to convince Josie that this relationship will never work. Is Victor who he says he is, and will Josie leave for Nigeria after hearing what Dr. Phil has to say?
Video.title: “Is My Nigerian Boyfriend Scamming Me?”

Video.summary: Parents Liz and Jim say their 16-year-old son, Nic, has a serious drug problem that has such a hold on him, they claim he even stole $75,000 in gold from his parents’ safe and spent it all on drugs! But, Liz says she is convinced that her husband is to blame. Liz accuses Jim of being a narcissist and misogynist, whose parenting philosophy is based on intimidation and abuse, and claims that is what drove their son to drugs. Jim denies Liz’s allegations, claiming she has gone to great lengths to paint him as a villain. He says she is the major problem, and calls her the world's lousiest parent and a doormat who helped enable Nic's deadly addictions.
Video.title: Teen Drug Problem (Part 1)

Video.summary: Shot in the face, disfigured for life, the former teen victim comes face to face with the girl who pulled the trigger. Can she forgive?
Video.title: The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face

Image.alt: The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face
The Girl Who Shot Me in the Face

Video.summary: Samantha and her sister Sarah say their mother, Stacy, is very successful as a special education teacher and behaviorist, but hey claim their mother has allowed their 33 year old brother Ben to live rent free for the past 2 years, while he lays by the pool. Stacy’s husband, Doug, says he moved out and refuses to come home until Stacy kicks his stepson out of the house. Ben insists he is not taking advantage of his mother’s hospitality and kindness; he is just stuck in a rut.
Video.title: Mom Needs to Cut the Cord

Image.alt: Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord
Mom Needs to Cut the Cord

Video.summary: Would you rather be right or happy? Dr. Phil talks with some right-fighters who can't settle their debate.
Video.title: I Am Right!

Video.summary: Are we raising a generation of ungrateful children? Melissa’s sisters say her sons are the most spoiled boys in North America. They get everything they want – all they have to do is whine. Melissa says her kids just like toys, and she thinks her sisters may be jealous of her lifestyle. Next, Dori admits that her 13-year-old son, Parker, is spoiled. Parker says he won't take no for an answer, and even has a strategy for getting everything he wants. Find out what's behind Dori's need to spoil Parker. Then, Joan says her 14-year-old daughter, Jacquie, is a snob, and her need for trendy clothes is turning her into a materialistic monster. Plus, Lauren spends her entire paycheck shopping, but with no money in her checking account, she’s afraid she could be headed for trouble. Her mom, Diane, says she's not worried, it's just Lauren's way of relaxing.
Video.title: Spoiled and Entitled

Video.summary: Christina, 21, and her younger sister, Carlie, 13, say they had a close relationship with their father, Michael, until two years ago when he decided to transition to a female. Michael was a police officer, soldier and loving parent who lived his life as a male for 40 years. Now, he wants his daughters to call him Mom, because he says Dad is gone, but they refuse. Michael, who prefers to be called Kayla, says she wants to explain to her daughters why she's living as a woman, but they've never given her the chance. The last time Christina saw her father was two years ago when he showed up at her work in lace and blue jeans to break the news that he's transgendered. When she sees Kayla onstage, does she greet her with open arms? Next, Carlie joins her sister and father. She says she wants her old dad back, but will she feel the same after seeing Kayla? Can the father-daughter bond be turned into a mother-daughter one? Be prepared for an emotional journey as two daughters struggle to understand how someone they loved so much could hurt them so badly.
Video.title: Daddy Drama

Video.summary: Dr. Phil debates viewers who disagree with the advice he gave to previous guests. One woman feels he had no right to push his view of adoption on pregnant 15-year-old, Alexandra. Another woman accuses Dr. Phil of bullying the participants on the Weight Loss Challenge and even accuses him of disliking fat people! What does Dr. Phil say about her inflated accusations? Then a 16-year-old bi-racial girl disputes Dr. Phil’s advice to a mother facing a racial identity crisis. How will these viewers fare when they go head to head with Dr. Phil?
Video.title: Debate Dr. Phil

Video.summary: One man says he caught his wife cheating – on audiotape – but she denies the accusations. Who’s really telling the truth? Dr. Phil gets to the bottom of the alleged affair on tape. Can this relationship survive without trust?
Video.title: Affair, Lies, & Audiotape

Video.summary: What happens when an unhealthy teen relationship threatens to tear a mother and daughter apart? Giesel says her 18-year-old daughter, Devynn, is obsessed with her boyfriend and is damaging a once promising future. Watch the drama unfold as a pregnancy scare rocks this teen's life.
Video.title: Teen Love Trauma

Video.summary: Christina and Jim are divorced, and their 21-year-old daughter, Tara, is living with her dad and his new wife who just happens to be the same age as Tara. Christina claims Jim was a horrible husband and a narcissist and she was forced to walk away from the family when Tara was just 13. Since then, she says Jim has let Tara get away with anything and now even allows her to work as a webcam model in order to help pay the bills! She says he is turning their daughter into a raging, angry monster with low self-esteem.
Video.title: "My Ex & My Daughter" (Part 1)

Video.summary: Everyone knows people that love to bicker. But what happens when the bickering escalates into verbal abuse and violence? Dr. Phil checks in with past guests Troy and Tammy whose explosive arguing is the primary dynamic in their marriage. Have they been able to break the cycle of drama or are they one step closer to divorce? Then—meet a single mother of five who worries that her children’s bickering may lead to fatal consequences! She thinks they’re out of control. So why is her daughter pointing the finger at her? Dr. Phil helps her end the fighting once and for all.
Video.title: The Bickersons

Video.summary: There comes a point when every child must grow up and face life on their own. For today’s guests, that time has come and gone and they want stay with Mom and Dad! You’ll meet Ryan, whose father says he’s borrowed over $10,000, and has trouble standing on his own two feet. And watch as Dr. Phil gives another young adult the tools she needs to create a life of her own. Find out what needs to happen before your offspring fly the coop.
Video.title: Moochers

Video.summary: Witness the “boomerang effect...” Dr. Phil talks to parents whose adult children keep coming back to live with, and off of them. One couple says their sons refuse to move out even though they are both almost 30-years-old. One 25- year-old is unemployed, has had 16 jobs in seven years, lives with his mother and even took her credits cards to charge up two thousand dollars worth of “stuff.” Dr. Phil helps these adult children address their issues so they can stop their destructive patterns.
Video.title: Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out

Image.alt: Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out
Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out
Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out
Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out
Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out
Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out
Adult Kids Who Won’t Move Out

Video.summary: Did you know that close to 14 million adults still live at home with their parents? Dr. Phil’s first guest is 30 years old, has 2 kids, and is still living with her mom! Her mom wants her out, so why won’t she leave? Then, meet one mother who made her son sign a contract to move out for good. The deadline has lapsed, but he won’t budge. How far will Dr. Phil go to push him out of the nest? Plus, meet a couple who opened their home to 21 family members over the last 7 years. Finally, their nephew came to stay for a couple of days…and two years later he’s still there!
Video.title: Kick 'Em Out!

Video.summary: Elizabeth and her husband, Bob, claim that Elizabeth’s 20-year-old daughter, Ali, is a “spoiled brat,” who refuses to go to college or get a job, has trashed their house and also steals from them. Dr. Phil has some tough questions for Ali about her behavior -- and for the parents, who admit they’ve enabled their daughter. Can Ali learn to stand on her own two feet?
Video.title: My Sloppy, Lazy, Mooching Daughter

Video.summary: Mike and Vicki say their 37-year-old son, Ryan, was once making millions running a successful real estate company, but for the past three months, he has been living in their garage, on their dime, along with his 24-year-old girlfriend, Rachel. The parents claim Ryan is a “freeloader” who sleeps all day, acts entitled, picks fights and even leaves them to care for his 5-year-old son. Ryan admits things are chaotic in his life but insists it’s not all his fault. Why does he say his parents are partly to blame for his business failing? Dr. Phil has some tough questions for Ryan about his behavior -- does he take any accountability? And, hear from Rachel -- is she happy with their living situation? Plus, Ryan isn’t the only one staying with mom and dad -- you won’t believe how many mouths Mike and Vicki say they are feeding! How can the parents stop the bleeding and take back control of their lives? And, is Ryan ready to step up to the plate and work toward living on his own?
Video.title: From Making Millions to Garage Life

Image.alt: From Making Millions to Garage Life
From Making Millions to Garage Life
From Making Millions to Garage Life
From Making Millions to Garage Life
From Making Millions to Garage Life
From Making Millions to Garage Life
From Making Millions to Garage Life

Video.summary: Cindy and Jim say they are fed up with their 35-year-old son, Brandon. They say he’s a moocher who refuses to get job, wears superhero costumes on a daily basis, and has caused $15,000 in damages to their property. Brandon says his parents don’t understand how his mind works. He admits to wearing superhero costumes and going on nightly “Batman” vigilante missions. Brandon says he also created a rapper persona named “Ceeza Caprice” and considers himself a “God MC” who can rap to any beat. Brandon’s sister, Harmony, says she blames her parents for Brandon’s behavior. She says they enable Brandon. She claims Brandon is a manipulator who sees it as a challenge to live off others.
Video.title: My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son

Image.alt: My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son
My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son
My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son
My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son
My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son
My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son
My Spoiled, Lazy, Adult Rapper Son

Video.summary: Alex says he will do absolutely anything to live his “bad and boujee” lifestyle. He admits he lies, cheats, and steals thousands of dollars from friends, family, and even strangers, just so he can live a lifestyle which includes shopping, tanning, and getting his hair done. Alex says he wants to become famous for his “twerking videos,” but until then, he believes his family should be taking care of him because he’s “just 26-year-old!” Alex’s mother, Kimberly, and sister, Elizabeth, say nothing about Alex’s life is glamorous, and claim he's addicted to drugs and a criminal. They claim Alex shows no remorse for any of his actions, even though he has stolen so much from his mother that she claims she will be homeless within the year! Can Dr. Phil get through to Alex? Will Alex ever change his “bad and boujee” ways? Find out!
Video.title: Desperate to be ‘Bad and Boujee’
